info.title: ClickUp Public API v3
info.description: This API is exposed to the public internet and is meant to be accessed by third-party integrations.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves the Channels in a Workspace.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: description_format
parameters.description: Format of the Channel description (Default: text/md).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Used to request the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

parameters.name: is_follower
parameters.description: Only return Channels the user is following.

parameters.name: include_closed
parameters.description: Include DMs/Group DMs that have been explicitly closed.

parameters.name: with_message_since
parameters.description: Only return Channels with at least one message since the given timestamp.

parameters.name: channel_types
parameters.description: Specify the types of Channels to return from the request.

post.description: This endpoint creates a new Channel not tied to a Space, Folder, or List. If a Channel with the specified name already exists it returns it.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

post.summary: Create a Channel on a Space, Folder, or List
post.description: This endpoint creates a Channel on a Space, Folder, or List. When a Channel already exists on the requested location, it returns it.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

post.description: This endpoint creates a new Direct Message between up to 15 users. If a Direct Message between those users already exists it returns it.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves a specific Channel given its ID.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified Channel.

parameters.name: description_format
parameters.description: Format of the Channel description (Default: text/md).

404.description: Returns when the requested {channelId} was not found.

patch.description: This endpoint updates a single Channel.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified Channel.

404.description: Returns when the requested {channelId} was not found when updating a Channel.

delete.description: This endpoint deletes a Channel. Applies to Channels tied to a Space, Folder, or List or not tied to locations.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified Channel.

404.description: Returns when the requested {channelId} was not found when deleting a Channel.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves followers of a specific Channel given its ID.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified Channel.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

404.description: Returns when the specified {channelId} was not found.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves members of a specific Channel given its ID.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified Channel.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves messages for a specified Channel.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Channel where the messages live.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

parameters.name: content_format
parameters.description: Format of the message content (Default: text/md).

404.description: Returns when the specified {channelId} was not found.

post.description: This endpoint creates a top level message.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Channel where the messages live.

404.description: Returns when {channelId} was not found.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

400.description: Returns when the request is invalid.

404.description: Returns when {messageId} was not found.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves the reactions to a message.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

404.description: Returns when the specified {messageId} was not found.

post.description: This endpoint creates a message reaction using lower case emoji names.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

400.description: Returns when the reaction {reaction} is not supported or already exists.

404.description: Returns when the message {messageId} is not found.

delete.description: This endpoint deletes a message reaction.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

parameters.name: reaction
parameters.description: The name of the reaction to be deleted.

400.description: Returns when the reaction {reaction} is not supported.

404.description: Returns when the message {messageId} or the reaction {reaction} is not found.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves the replies to a message.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

parameters.name: content_format
parameters.description: Format of the message content (Default: text/md).

404.description: Returns when the specified {messageId} was not found.

post.description: This endpoint creates a reply message.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves tagged users for a message.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the specified message.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

404.description: Returns when the specified {messageId} was not found.

get.description: Return a list of attachments for the specified entity, including a task or File type Custom Field.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: The entity type. Options include `attachments` for tasks and `custom_fields` for a File type Custom Field.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: The ID of the task or Custom Field.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

400.description: Returns when request was invalid when fetching attachments.

404.description: Returns when {entityId} not found when fetching attachments.

post.description:
Upload an attachment to a task or to a file type Custom Field. \
 \
Once a file has been uploaded to a `custom_fields` entity, use [Set Custom Field Value](ref:SetCustomFieldValue) to associate the uploaded file with a task. \
 \
To retrieve the ID of a Custom Field, you can use [Get List Custom Fields](red:GetAccessibleCustomFields) or [Get task](ref:GetTask).

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: The entity type. Options include `attachments` for tasks or `custom_fields` for a Files Custom Field.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: The ID of the task or Custom Field.

404.description: Returns when {entityId} not found.

patch.summary: Update privacy and access of an object or location
patch.description: Update the privacy and access settings of an object or location in the Workspace. Note that sharing an item may incur charges.

parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: ObjectType

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: The ID of the object to share.

post.description: Create Workspace-level audit logs. Audit logs can only be created by the Workspace owner on Enterprise Plans.

get.description:
Get the ID of each post subtype (Announcement, Discussion, Idea, and Update) for your Workspace, to use when [sending a message](https://developer.clickup.com/reference/createchatmessage) with `type: post`. \
 \
Subtype IDs are unique to each Workspace.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: ID of the logged-in user's Workspace.

get.description: View the Docs in your Workspace. You can only view information of Docs you can access.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter results to Docs created by the user with the given Doc ID.

parameters.name: creator
parameters.description: Filter results to Docs created by the user with the given user ID.

parameters.name: deleted
parameters.description: Filter results to return deleted Docs.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Filter results to return archived Docs.

parameters.name: parent_id
parameters.description: Filter results to children of a parent Doc with the given parent Doc ID.

parameters.name: parent_type
parameters.description: Filter results to children of the given parent Doc type. For example, `SPACE`, `FOLDER`, `LIST`, `EVERYTHING`, `WORKSPACE`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to fetch for this page.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results.

parameters.name: next_cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results. [deprecated]

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: doc_id
parameters.description: The ID of the doc.

404.description: Doc not found

get.description: View the PageListing for a Doc.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: doc_id
parameters.description: The ID of the doc.

parameters.name: max_page_depth
parameters.description: The maximum depth to retrieve pages and subpages. Use `-1` to return pages and subpages with no depth limit.

404.description: PageListing not found

get.summary: Fetch Pages belonging to a Doc
get.description: View the pages belonging to a Doc.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: doc_id
parameters.description: The ID of the doc.

parameters.name: max_page_depth
parameters.description: The maximum depth to retrieve pages and subpages. Use `-1` to return pages and subpages with no depth limit.

parameters.name: content_format
parameters.description: The format to return the page content in. For example, `text/md` for markdown or `text/plain` for plain text.

post.description: Create a page in a Doc.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: doc_id
parameters.description: The ID of the doc.

get.description: View the information about a page in a Doc. Due to markdown format limitations, some content elements [will not be displayed exactly as they appear in ClickUp](doc:docsimportexportlimitations/).

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: doc_id
parameters.description: The ID of the doc.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.description: The ID of the page

parameters.name: content_format
parameters.description: The format to return the page content in. For example, `text/md` for markdown or `text/plain` for plain text.

404.description: Page not found

put.description: Edit a page in a Doc.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: doc_id
parameters.description: The ID of the doc.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.description: The ID of the page

put.summary: Move a task to a new List
put.description:
Move a task to a new List. For Tasks in Multiple Lists, this endpoint only moves the task to a new home List.
 
[Learn more](doc:move-a-task-to-a-new-list) about the options available when moving a task to a new List.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The ID of the requested Task.

parameters.name: list_id
parameters.description: The ID of the List to move the task to.

400.description: Returns when the request is invalid.

404.description: Returns when the task or list is not found.

patch.summary: Update task time estimates by user
patch.description:
Replace the time estimates on a task for specified assignees.
 
Assignees must be currently assigned to the task, or use `assignee: unassigned` to set a time estimates for unassigned work.
 
You can set up to 10 estimates per request.
 
***Note:** This endpoint is only available on our Business Plan or above.*

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The ID of the task. This parameter supports task IDs and Custom task IDs.

requestBody.description: Array of time estimates per assignee to update (replaces existing, leaves others unchanged)

400.description: Invalid request parameters, validation error, or not entitled to use time estimates per assignee feature

404.description: Task not found or not authorized

put.description:
Replaces all time estimates for a task with the provided time estimates. Any existing time estimates not included in the request will be removed. 
 
Assignees must be currently assigned to the task, or use `assignee: unassigned` to set a time estimates for unassigned work.
 
You can set up to 10 estimates per request.
 
***Note:** This endpoint is only available on our Business Plan or above.*

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Workspace.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The ID of the task. This parameter supports task IDs and Custom task IDs.

requestBody.description: Array of time estimates per assignee

400.description: Invalid request parameters, validation error, or not entitled to use time estimates per assignee feature

404.description: Task not found or not authorized

allOf.description: The list of attachments for {entityId}.

next_cursor.description: The next cursor to use for pagination.

date_updated.description: The date & time the attachment was updated (Unix epoch milliseconds timestamp).

date_created.description: The date & time the attachment was created (Unix epoch milliseconds timestamp).

mime_type.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

parent_entity_type.description: The type of entity the attachment belongs to.

parent_id.description: The ID of the entity the attachment belongs to.

size.description: The size in bytes of the attachment.

signed.description: The signed status of the attachment.

thumbnail_small.description: The small size thumbnail url of the attachment.

thumbnail_medium.description: The medium size thumbnail url of the attachment.

thumbnail_large.description: The large size thumbnail url of the attachment.

user_id.description: The ID of the user who created the attachment.

status.description: The HTTP Status code for the error

trace_id.description: Unique trace ID for tracking the request. Used for troubleshooting errors

timestamp.description: The time of the error (Unix epoch milliseconds timestamp).

filename.description: Override the filename of the attachment

filter.description: A filter containing the criteria to filter logs by.

applicability.description: Type of logs to filter by. Options include `auth-and-security`, `custom-fields`, `hierarchy-activity` and `user-activity`. Most use cases will use `auth-and-security`.

pagination.description: The pagination request determines where logs should start and how many to return.

userId.description: The user ID. This can be a single user or multiple.

userEmail.description: User emails. This can be a single email or multiple.

eventType.description: Types of events to filter by. Options include `USER_LOGIN`, `USER_LOGOUT`, `CHANGE_2FA`, `CHANGE_PASSWORD`, `CHANGE_EMAIL`, `JOIN_WORKSPACE`, `LEAVE_WORKSPACE`, `REMOVE_FROM_WORKSPACE`, `RESET_PASSWORD`, `ROLE_CHANGE`, `SCIM_PROVISION`, `SCIM_DEPROVISION`, `SCIM_UPDATE`, `ADVANCED_SETTINGS_UPDATED`, `CHANGE_ROLE_PERMISSIONS`, `INVITE_TO_WORKSPACE`, `CHANGE_USER_ROLE`, `CHANGE_SSO_POLICY`, `CHANGE_2FA_POLICY`, `CUSTOM_ROLE_CREATED`, `CUSTOM_ROLE_UPDATED`, `CUSTOM_ROLE_DELETED`, `SCIM_GROUP_CREATED`, `SCIM_GROUP_UPDATED`, `SCIM_GROUP_DELETED`, `TEAM_CREATED`, `TEAM_DELETED`, `TEAM_EDITED`, `TEAM_MEMBER_ADDED`, `TEAM_MEMBER_REMOVED`, `SSO_CONFIG_UPDATED`, `TASK_CREATED`, `TASK_ARCHIVED`, `TASK_DELETED`, `TASK_RESTORED`, `TASK_UNARCHIVED`, `TASK_STATUS_CHANGED`, `TASK_PRIORITY_CHANGED`, `TASK_ASSIGNEES_CHANGED`, and `TASK_CUSTOM_FIELD_VALUES_CHANGED`.

eventStatus.description: Status of events you're filtering by. Options include `success`, `failed`, `started`, `completed`, `error`, `system_error`.

startTime.description: The start time to begin paginating your logs.

endTime.description: The end time to begin paginating your logs.

pageRows.description: The number of rows to return.

pageTimestamp.description: The timestamp to index on. This should be the timestamp of the last row on the previous page. If you are requesting the first page, this should be the current timestamp.

pageDirection.description: Pagination direction. This should be set to `before` for most use cases. Options include `before` and `after`.

allOf.description: The list of Channels.

next_cursor.description: The next cursor to use for pagination.

description.description: The full Description of the Channel.

creator.description: ID of the user who created this room

created_at.description: Timestamp of when this room was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last update in this room - Deprecated in favor of latest_comment_at

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID of the chat room

latest_comment_at.description: Timestamp of the last comment in this room. Optional in the case of a new room with no comments yet

is_canonical_channel.description: Is this the canonical channel for the parent location?

is_hidden.description: Has the user hidden this room from their sidebar? Currently only an option for DM/Group DM.

default_view.description: Data about the default view that the room should open in

counts.description: Counts relevant to the user requesting the chat room

chat_room_category.description: The category of the chat room, e.g. a welcome channel, (in the future, maybe) a ClickBot channel, etc.

canvas_id.description: The id of the canvas owned by the chat room, if any

links.description: The links to elements of the Channel.

ChatSubcategoryType.description: Type of channel (chat, project, etc.) aka subcategory_type

date.description: When the user last read comments to this object, set in the past to mark comments as unread. Should be a timestamp in milliseconds.

version.description: The version of the object when the last read at object was last updated.

has_unread.description: Whether the user has unread comments on this object. (Optional)

num_unread.description: How many unread comments there are on this object. (Optional)

latest_comment_at.description: The date of the latest comment on this object. (Optional)

badge_count.description: The badge count for this object. (Optional)

thread_count.description: The number of unread threads on this object. (Optional)

mention_count.description: The number of mentions on this object. (Optional)

last_updated_at.description: The date of the last update to this object. (Optional)

ObjectType.description: Any object that can be shared in a Workspace. For example, `customField`, `dashboard`, `folder`, `goal`, `goalFolder`, `list`, `space`, `task`, and `view`.

members.description: The link to the members of the Channel.

followers.description: The link to the followers of the Channel.

status.description: The HTTP Status code for the error

trace_id.description: Unique trace ID for tracking the request. Used for troubleshooting errors

timestamp.description: The time of the error (Unix epoch milliseconds timestamp).

description.description: The description for the Chat being created.

name.description: The name for the Chat being created.

topic.description: The topic of the Chat being created.

user_ids.description: Optionally specify unique user IDs, up to 100.

visibility.description: The visibility of the Chat being created. If not specified, the Chat is PUBLIC.

ChatPublicApiChatChannelsControllerCreat.description: The existing Channel that is matching the creation request.

location.description: The updated location of the chat: space, folder, or list

name.description: The updated name of the Chat.

topic.description: The updated topic of the Chat.

type.description: The type of the location. Must be a space, folder, or list.

The list of Channel members.

user_ids.description: The unique user IDs of participants in the direct message Chat, up to 15. A Self DM is created when no user IDs are provided

allOf.description: Successfully retrieved the list of tasks for a specific List.

next_cursor.description: The next cursor to use for pagination.

custom_id.description: The custom id of a task

description.description: The description of a task.  Can be formatted in either plain text or markdown.Some references may be missing when asked as plain text.

due_date.description: The due date of the task in unix epoch time ms.

folder_id.description: The folder that the task belongs in.

list_id.description: ID of the main list that the task belongs to.

links.description: Links to components of a task. This includes subtasks, checklists, fields, attachments, related users and such.

parent_task_id.description: The direct parent task id of this.

priority.description: The priority of the task. The lower the more urgent.

root_parent_task_id.description: The top most level parent task id. Relevant with nested subtasks

space_id.description: The space that the task belongs in.

sprint_points.description: How many sprint points that this task is estimated to be.

start_date.description: When the task has been marked as started in unix epoch time ms.

task_type_id.description: The custom task type that this is.

time_estimate.description: How long the task is estimated to take in ms.

workspace_id.description: The workspace that the task belongs in.

assignees.description: A link to the users that are assigned to this task

attachments.description: A link to all the attachments that are a part of the task

checklists.description: The link to the checklists of the task

doc_relationships.description: A link to document relationships for the task

custom_fields.description: The link to the custom fields of a task

list_relationships.description: A link to list relationships for the task

point_estimates_by_user.description: A link to the point estimates by user

subtasks.description: A link to the subtasks of a task

tags.description: A link to the tags of a task

time_estimates_by_user.description: A link to the time estimates by user

watchers.description: A link to the users that are watching this task

status.description: The HTTP Status code for the error

trace_id.description: Unique trace ID for tracking the request. Used for troubleshooting errors

timestamp.description: The time of the error (Unix epoch milliseconds timestamp).

user.description: A user that is related to the task.

type.description: How the user is related to the task eg. watcher, assignee etc.

task_id.description: ID of the task the checklist belongs to.

items.description: The link to the items of the checklist.

value.description: Raw representation of the current Custom Field value when available.

group.description: The Group assigned to the task

type.description: The type of the group assignee

move_custom_fields.description: Add Custom Fields from the current List to the new List.

custom_fields_to_move.description: An array of Custom Fields IDs to move. This parameter is not required to move all Custom Fields to the new List.

status_mappings.description:
Map the current List statuses to the statuses in the new List. You only need to include the ID of the task's current status and the ID of it's new status.
 
**Note:** This parameter is required if the task's current status is not available in the new List.

source_status_id.description: The ID of a status from the task's current List.

destination_status_id.description: The ID of the respective status in the new List.

TaskPublicApiTaskControllerMoveTask200Re.description: Successfully updated the task home list

allOf.description: Successfully retrieved the task's related lists.

type.description: The type of list relationship for a task.

Successfully retrieved the task's subtasks.

assignee.description: The user ID of the assignee, or `unassigned` for time estimates that are not assigned to anyone.

oneOf.description: The user ID of the task assignee.

time.description: The time estimate in milliseconds. Must be a non-negative integer.

total_time_estimate.description: The total time estimate across all assignees in milliseconds

assignee_estimates.description: A map of assignee user IDs to their respective time estimates in milliseconds. The key "unassigned" represents time not assigned to any specific user.

total_time_estimate.description: The total time estimate across all assignees after the update, in milliseconds

updated_estimates.description: A map of assignee user IDs to their updated time estimates in milliseconds. Only includes assignees whose estimates were modified by this request.

allOf.description: Successfully retrieved the specified task's watchers.

start_date.description: Start of the date range filter in Unix milliseconds (UTC). Returns time entries that overlap with this range - includes entries that started before this date but ended during or after it, as well as active timers (entries with no end time). Defaults to 30 days before end_date. Maximum range is 30 days.

end_date.description: End of the date range filter in Unix milliseconds (UTC). Returns time entries that overlap with this range - includes entries that started during or before this date and ended after it, as well as active timers. Defaults to current time. Maximum range is 30 days.

direction.description: Sort direction. asc = ascending (earliest to latest), desc = descending (latest to earliest)

kind.description: The type of ID. Options are `user` or `group`.

id.description: The ID of the user or user group (Team) you wish to give, remove, or edit permissions.

permission_level.description: The permission level you wish to give to user or user group (Team). `1`=read, `3` = comment, `4`= edit, `5`= create, and `null`= remove access.

from_group_id.description: If the user ACL entry was expanded from a group, this is the group's ID

from_parent_id.description: If set, this ACL entry was expanded from a parent with this id.  Will be undefined if parent is not accessible.

calculated.description: If set, this ACL entry was calculated from the user's attributes (e.g. the user is the owner of the object)

start_date.description: Filter records with start date on or after this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

end_date.description: Filter records with end date on or before this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

updated_since.description: Filter records updated on or after this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

reasons.description: Filter by specific time off reasons.

allOf.description: The list of personal time off records, ordered by ID.

next_cursor.description: The next cursor to use for pagination.

id.description: Unique identifier for the time off record

member_id.description: User ID this time off applies to

start.description: Start time of time off in milliseconds since epoch

end.description: End time of time off in milliseconds since epoch

updated_at.description: When this time off record was last updated in milliseconds since epoch

status.description: The HTTP Status code for the error

trace_id.description: Unique trace ID for tracking the request. Used for troubleshooting errors

timestamp.description: The time of the error (Unix epoch milliseconds timestamp).

start.description: Start date of time off as timestamp at midnight UTC (milliseconds since epoch). Time components are truncated to date only.

end.description: End date of time off as timestamp at midnight UTC (milliseconds since epoch). Time components are truncated to date only.

id.description: Unique identifier for the time off record

member_id.description: User ID this time off applies to

start.description: Start date of time off as timestamp at midnight UTC (milliseconds since epoch). Time components are truncated to date only.

end.description: End date of time off as timestamp at midnight UTC (milliseconds since epoch). Time components are truncated to date only.

updated_at.description: When this time off record was last updated in milliseconds since epoch

private.description: The privacy of an object or location.

entries.description: The user or user group (Team) you wish to give, remove, or edit permissions.
